summary_ja: 英国では実技試験の予約待ちが5か月超と長期化し、昨年は6万4,500件の無断キャンセル（ノーショー）があった。6月9日から、受験者は当初予約した場所に最も近い3つの試験センターにしか日程変更できなくなり、遠方を取って後から近くに替える行為を抑止する。教習生や指導員は依然として待ち時間の長さに不満を持ち、業界団体は対策の効果に懐疑的だ。

sentences.text: Learner drivers are now only able to swap their test to the three centres nearest to their original booking location in a bid to cut down waiting times.
sentences.grammar_ja: in a bid to ～「～しようとして」はニュースでよく使う目的表現。only able to swap は「変更できるのは～だけ」という制限の言い方。

sentences.text: It comes as official figures shared exclusively with the BBC suggest no-one turned up to take 64,500 practical driving tests last year.
sentences.grammar_ja: It comes as ～「それは～という状況の中で起きている」で背景を示す。shared exclusively with は「独占的に共有された」報道表現。

sentences.text: The average wait for practical driving tests across Britain are longer than five months.
sentences.grammar_ja: 主語は The average wait（単数）なので本来は is が自然だが、本文は are のまま。across Britain は「英国全体で」。

sentences.text: The new rules will stop learners booking the soonest test available anywhere, then making a series of swaps to get a slot closer to home.
sentences.grammar_ja: stop + 人 + doing「～するのを止めさせる」。a series of swaps で「何度も予約変更する」流れを表す。

sentences.text: Learner driver Emma told the BBC she was waking up at 05:30 every Monday to try to book a test only to find herself in a queue of thousands.
sentences.grammar_ja: only to find ～「すると～だと分かった」で予想外の結果。in a queue of thousands は「数千人の列に並ぶ」。

sentences.text: She now has a test in seven months time.
sentences.grammar_ja: in seven months' time / in seven months time で「7か月後に」。所有格 ' は省略されることもある。

sentences.text: In England the wait time for a driving test is 22.7 weeks, Scotland 22.9 weeks and in Wales 17.3 weeks, according to figures provided to the BBC by the Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA) for April 2026.
sentences.grammar_ja: according to ～「～によると」で出典を示す。地名の並列で the wait time is を繰り返さない省略構文。

sentences.text: Last year, 1,998,608 driving tests were booked in the UK but no one turned up for 64,500 of them meaning 3.2% were wasted, according to the DVSA.
sentences.grammar_ja: turn up for ～「～に現れる・受験する」。meaning 3.2% were wasted は結果を付け足す分詞構文。

sentences.text: Some of these were booked by third party resellers using bots with the intention of charging inflated prices but were unable to sell them, the BBC understands.
sentences.grammar_ja: the BBC understands は「BBCの取材によると」という報道者の言い回し。with the intention of ～ing「～する意図で」。

sentences.text: The number of no shows last year was higher than the 52,000 recorded the previous year.
sentences.grammar_ja: no shows は名詞「無断欠席」。higher than the 52,000 recorded ～ は過去分詞で「記録された数」という意味の省略。

sentences.text: Emma, not her real name, is 21 and has been learning to drive in West London for nearly a year.
sentences.grammar_ja: not her real name は挿入句「仮名である」。has been learning は「ずっと練習し続けている」現在完了進行形。

sentences.text: "Some of my friends who need to drive for work were booking tests at test centres not local to them in areas that they hadn't really driven before...just so that they could get the test and just try and pass as fast as they could," she said.
sentences.grammar_ja: just so that ～「～できるように」目的。not local to them「彼らの近くではない」。try and pass は try to pass と同様の口語。

sentences.text: Emma managed to book a test near to where she lives but it is not for seven months.
sentences.grammar_ja: managed to ～「何とか～できた」。near to where she lives は関係副詞 where を含む場所の節。

sentences.text: "I'm then paying for lessons every week, which is fine, it's good to have the practice, but when you've got so long until your test, it's just a little bit of a waste of money and a massive time burden," she said.
sentences.grammar_ja: which is fine で前の内容を受ける非制限用法。a waste of money / a time burden は collocations。

sentences.text: Emma's driving instructor Donovan has been using his local test centre for 10 years.
sentences.grammar_ja: has been using は10年続く習慣・経験を表す現在完了進行形。

sentences.text: "At one point, I didn't have a test there for six months, simply because none of my students could get one at booking there," he said.
sentences.grammar_ja: at one point「ある時期には」。get one at booking there は「予約時に枠を取れなかった」という意味。

sentences.text: "Effectively, you had people booking tests in Scotland just to get the date and then changing it to London when one became available," he said.
sentences.grammar_ja: Effectively「実質的には」。you had people booking は you had + 人 + doing で「人々が～していた状況だった」。

sentences.text: He hopes the changes "will reduce people booking tests that they have no intention of taking" and "free up a bit more space on the booking system".
sentences.grammar_ja: have no intention of taking「受けるつもりがない」。free up「空きを作る・解放する」。

sentences.text: However, Carly Brookfield, chief executive of the Driving Instructors Association, says the industry "doesn't have a huge amount of confidence that any of these measures are realistically fixing the booking system problem".
sentences.grammar_ja: chief executive of ～「～の最高責任者」。confidence that 節「～という確信」。

sentences.text: Ann Harvey contacted BBC Your Voice last month after her teenage son had failed to get a test in Reading and finally sat his driving test in Bury St Edmunds, more than 130 miles away.
sentences.grammar_ja: had failed to get は過去完了「（その時点まで）取れなかった」。more than 130 miles away は距離の後置修飾。

sentences.text: "I was also shocked by the number of no shows listed at Bury St Edmunds.
sentences.grammar_ja: shocked by「～にショックを受けた」。listed at は「その会場に記録されていた」無断欠席者。

sentences.text: There should be a penalty for not turning up," she said.
sentences.grammar_ja: There should be ～「～があるべきだ」提案・義務の弱い表現。penalty for not turning up「欠席への罰則」。

sentences.text: Beverley Warmington, DVSA's chief executive, said: "The location restrictions introduced on 9 June will help to deter bookings at locations where learners do not intend to take their test."
sentences.grammar_ja: introduced on 9 June は過去分詞の後置修飾。deter ～ from「～を思いとどまらせる」だがここでは deter bookings。

sentences.text: She added that the DVSA was "determined to reduce waiting times further" and had delivered more than 217,000 additional tests between June 2025 and April 2026 partly using military driving examiners.
sentences.grammar_ja: be determined to ～「～する決意だ」。partly using ～ は手段を示す分詞構文。

sentences.text: From 12 May, only learner drivers can book their own tests, not instructors.
sentences.grammar_ja: From 12 May で施行日を示す。not instructors は「指導員は（予約できない）」という対比の省略。

sentences.text: An ex-police officer says illegal number plates are helping criminals evade justice.
sentences.grammar_ja: ex-「元～」。evade justice「法の裁きを逃れる」。helping は進行形で現在の傾向。

sentences.text: Councillors discuss proposals for a 20mph limit across Dorchester's roads, with one exception.
sentences.grammar_ja: proposals for ～「～に関する提案」。with one exception「1つだけ例外を除いて」。

sentences.text: Motoring group RAC warns pump prices could keep rising if there is no resolution to the Iran war.
sentences.grammar_ja: warn (that) ～「～だと警告する」that 省略。resolution to「～の解決」。could keep rising「上がり続ける可能性」。

sentences.text: Scottish ministers have urged the UK government to grant them the authority to introduce a graduated driving licence system for new drivers, aiming to address the high casualty rates among young people on the roads.
sentences.grammar_ja: urge 人 to ～「～するよう強く求める」。aiming to address は目的を示す分詞構文。among young people「若者の間で」。

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 教習生、運転免許を取得中の人
vocabulary.example: Learner drivers are now only able to swap their test to the three centres nearest to their original booking location.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 無断欠席、予約したのに現れないこと
vocabulary.example: No one turned up to take 64,500 practical driving tests last year.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: ～しようとして、～を目的として
vocabulary.example: New rules were introduced in a bid to cut down waiting times.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: （予約などを）取り替える、変更する
vocabulary.example: Learners were making a series of swaps to get a slot closer to home.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 待ち時間、予約が取れるまでの期間
vocabulary.example: The DVSA was determined to reduce waiting times further.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 第三者の再販業者、仲介業者
vocabulary.example: Some tests were booked by third party resellers using bots.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: （悪い行為を）思いとどまらせる、抑止する
vocabulary.example: Location restrictions will help to deter bookings where learners do not intend to take their test.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 空きを作る、余裕を生み出す
vocabulary.example: He hopes the changes will free up a bit more space on the booking system.

grammar_points.title: It comes as + clause
grammar_points.rule: Use 'It comes as...' to introduce news against a background fact or trend.
grammar_points.example: It comes as official figures suggest no-one turned up to take 64,500 practical driving tests last year.
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 「それは～という状況の中で起きている」と、新しい出来事を背景データと結びつけて報じる定番のニュース構文。

grammar_points.title: stop + object + -ing
grammar_points.rule: After 'stop', use a gerund (-ing) to show what action is prevented.
grammar_points.example: The new rules will stop learners booking the soonest test available anywhere.
grammar_points.meaning_ja: stop + 人 + doing で「（人が）～するのを防ぐ・やめさせる」という意味。prevent と似た使い方。

grammar_points.title: only to + infinitive
grammar_points.rule: Use 'only to + verb' to show an unexpected or disappointing result.
grammar_points.example: She was waking up at 05:30 every Monday to try to book a test only to find herself in a queue of thousands.
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 努力や行動のあとに、思いがけない（often negative）結果が起きたときに only to を使う。

grammar_points.title: according to + source
grammar_points.rule: Use 'according to' to attribute information to a person, organisation, or data.
grammar_points.example: In England the wait time is 22.7 weeks, according to figures provided by the DVSA.
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 「～によると」で情報の出所を明示し、報道の客観性を示す。公式統計や当局の発表で頻出。
